'''田中 頼庸'''（たなか よりつね、[[1839年]]（[[天保]]7年）[[5月]] - [[1897年]]（[[明治]]30年）[[4月10日]]）は、[[幕末]]から明治時代の[[神道|神道家]]、[[国学|国学者]]、[[薩摩藩]]士である。通称は藤八。号は雲岫又は梅屋。
== 経歴・人物 == [[大山綱良]]を叔父に持つ薩摩藩の一家に生まれる。幼年の頃に薩摩藩士となり、[[鹿児島城]]で[[重野安繹]]、[[今藤新左衛門]]らと共に[[文学]]の秀才と名付けられた。後に[[高崎正風]]の従兄弟である[[高崎五六]]と親交を持った事で、正風は頼庸の父である綱良が属した若手の薩摩藩の組である「[[精忠組|誠忠組]]」をに入ったが、頼庸は辞退する。
その後、[[島津久光]]が執行した[[文久の改革]]により活動の場を[[京都]]に移し、[[儒教|儒学]]や[[医学]]等を学んだ。後に国学を専攻し、[[1867年]]（[[慶応]]3年）に薩摩に戻った。[[明治維新|維新]]後の[[1871年]]（明治4年）に[[神祇省]]に入省し、[[教部大録]]や[[伊勢神宮]]の[[禰宜]]となった。その後、[[教導職|大教正]]や[[神道事務局]]の副管長も務める。[[1882年]]（明治15年）には管長に昇格した事により独立し、禰宜を辞職する。同時期に[[常世長胤]]や[[出雲派]]の[[千家尊福]]を批判する等、一躍有名となった。
== 主な著作物 ==
=== 主著 ===
=== 主著 ===
* 『校訂日本紀』 * 『校訂古事記』 * 『神徳論』 * 『校訂日本紀』 * 『校訂古事記』 * 『神徳論』 * 『（神宮）祭神略記』
=== その他の著書 ===
* 『三則演義』 * 『梅の屋文集』 * 『校訂古語拾遺』 * 『（神宮）祭神提要』 * 『語書』
== 出典 ==
* デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus（[[講談社]]）『[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 田中頼庸]』- [[コトバンク]] * 朝日日本歴史人物事典（[[朝日新聞]]出版）『[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 田中頼庸]』- [[コトバンク]] * [[ブリタニカ百科事典|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典]] 小項目事典（ブリタニカ・ジャパン）『[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 田中頼庸]』- [[コトバンク]] * 美術人名事典（[[思文閣出版|思文閣]]）『[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 田中頼庸]』- [[コトバンク]]

'''田中 頼庸'''（たなか よりつね、[[1839年]]（[[天保]]7年）[[5月]] - [[1897年]]（[[明治]]30年）[[4月10日]]）は、[[幕末]]から明治時代の[[神道|神道家]]、[[国学|国学者]]、[[薩摩藩]]士である。通称は藤八。号は雲岫又は梅屋。

revision.comment: 加筆・修正。
revision.text:
'''田中 頼庸'''（たなか よりつね、[[1836年]]（[[天保]]7年）[[5月]] - [[1897年]]（[[明治]]30年）[[4月10日]]）は、[[幕末]]から明治時代の[[神道|神道家]]、[[国学|国学者]]、[[薩摩藩]]士である。通称は藤八。号は雲岫又は梅屋。
== 経歴・人物 == [[大山綱良]]を叔父に持つ薩摩藩の一家に生まれる。幼年の頃に薩摩藩士となり、[[鹿児島城]]で[[重野安繹]]、[[今藤新左衛門]]らと共に[[文学]]の秀才と名付けられた。後に[[高崎正風]]の従兄弟である[[高崎五六]]と親交を持った事で、正風は頼庸の父である綱良が属した若手の薩摩藩の組である「[[精忠組|誠忠組]]」に入ったが、頼庸は辞退する。
その後、[[島津久光]]が執行した[[文久の改革]]により活動の場を[[京都]]に移し、[[儒教|儒学]]や[[医学]]等を学んだ。後に国学を専攻し、[[1867年]]（[[慶応]]3年）に薩摩に戻った。[[明治維新|維新]]後の[[1871年]]（明治4年）に[[神祇省]]に入省し、[[教部大録]]や[[伊勢神宮]]の[[禰宜]]となった。その後、[[教導職|大教正]]や[[神道事務局]]の副管長も務める。[[1882年]]（明治15年）には管長に昇格した事により独立し、禰宜を辞職する。同時期に[[常世長胤]]や[[出雲派]]の[[千家尊福]]を批判する等、一躍有名となった。
== 主な著作物 ==
=== 主著 ===
* 『校訂日本紀』 * 『校訂古事記』 * 『神徳論』 * 『（神宮）祭神略記』
=== その他の著書 ===
* 『三則演義』 * 『梅の屋文集』 * 『校訂古語拾遺』 * 『（神宮）祭神提要』 * 『語書』
== 出典 ==
* デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus（[[講談社]]）『[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 田中頼庸]』- [[コトバンク]] * 朝日日本歴史人物事典（[[朝日新聞]]出版）『[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 田中頼庸]』- [[コトバンク]] * [[ブリタニカ百科事典|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典]] 小項目事典（ブリタニカ・ジャパン）『[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 田中頼庸]』- [[コトバンク]] * 美術人名事典（[[思文閣出版|思文閣]]）『[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 田中頼庸]』- [[コトバンク]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://inakaseikatsu.blogspot.com/2018/03/blog-post_15.html 田中頼庸と幕末の国学―なぜ鹿児島には神代三陵が全てあるのか？（その8）- 南薩日乗]

{{DEFAULTSORT:たなか よりつね}} [[Category:国学者]] [[Category:19世紀の学者]] [[Category:明治時代の神職]] [[Category:伊勢神宮の祠官]] [[Category:教部省の人物]] [[Category:日本の官僚 (1868-1949)]] [[Category:神道に関連する人物]] [[Category:鹿児島県出身の人物]] [[Category:幕末薩摩藩の人物]] [[Category:1836年生]] [[Category:1897年没]]

'''田中 頼庸'''（たなか よりつね、[[1836年]]（[[天保]]7年）[[5月]] - [[1897年]]（[[明治]]30年）[[4月10日]]）は、[[幕末]]から明治時代の[[神道|神道家]]、[[国学|国学者]]、[[薩摩藩]]士である。通称は藤八。号は雲岫又は梅屋。
== 経歴・人物 == [[大山綱良]]を叔父に持つ薩摩藩の一家に生まれる。幼年の頃に薩摩藩士となり、[[鹿児島城]]で[[重野安繹]]、[[今藤新左衛門]]らと共に[[文学]]の秀才と名付けられた。後に[[高崎正風]]の従兄弟である[[高崎五六]]と親交を持った事で、正風は頼庸の父である綱良が属した若手の薩摩藩の組である「[[精忠組|誠忠組]]」に入ったが、頼庸は辞退する。
その後、[[島津久光]]が執行した[[文久の改革]]により活動の場を[[京都]]に移し、[[儒教|儒学]]や[[医学]]等を学んだ。後に国学を専攻し、[[1867年]]（[[慶応]]3年）に薩摩に戻った。[[明治維新|維新]]後の[[1871年]]（明治4年）に[[神祇省]]に入省し、[[教部大録]]や[[伊勢神宮]]の[[禰宜]]となった。
さらにその後、[[教導職|大教正]]や[[神道事務局]]の副管長も務める。
[[1882年]]（明治15年）には管長に昇格した事により独立し、禰宜を辞職する。同時期に[[常世長胤]]や[[出雲派]]の[[千家尊福]]を批判する等、一躍有名となった。
== 主な著作物 == === 主著 === * 『校訂日本紀』 * 『校訂古事記』 * 『神徳論』 * 『(神宮）祭神略記』
=== その他の著書 === * 『三則演義』 * 『梅の屋文集』 * 『校訂古語拾遺』 * 『(神宮）祭神提要』 * 『語書』
== 出典 == * デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus（[[講談社]]）『[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 田中頼庸]』- [[コトバンク]] * 朝日日本歴史人物事典（[[朝日新聞]]出版）『[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 田中頼庸]』- [[コトバンク]] * [[ブリタニカ百科事典|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典]] 小項目事典（ブリタニカ・ジャパン）『[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 田中頼庸]』- [[コトバンク]] * 美術人名事典（[[思文閣出版|思文閣]]）『[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 田中頼庸]』- [[コトバンク]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://inakaseikatsu.blogspot.com/2018/03/blog-post_15.html 田中頼庸と幕末の国学―なぜ鹿児島には神代三陵が全てあるのか？（その8）- 南薩日乗]

revision.comment: /* 経歴・人物 */
revision.text:
'''田中 頼庸'''（たなか よりつね、[[1836年]]（[[天保]]7年）[[5月]] - [[1897年]]（[[明治]]30年）[[4月10日]]）は、[[幕末]]から明治時代の[[神道|神道家]]、[[国学|国学者]]、[[薩摩藩]]士である。通称は藤八。号は雲岫又は梅屋。
== 経歴・人物 == [[大山綱良]]を叔父に持つ薩摩藩の一家に生まれる。幼年の頃に薩摩藩士となり、[[鹿児島城]]で[[重野安繹]]、[[今藤新左衛門]]らと共に[[文学]]の秀才と名付けられた。後に[[高崎正風]]の従兄弟である[[高崎五六]]と親交を持った事で、正風は頼庸の叔父である綱良が属した若手の薩摩藩の組である「[[精忠組|誠忠組]]」に入ったが、頼庸は辞退する。
その後、[[島津久光]]が執行した[[文久の改革]]により活動の場を[[京都]]に移し、[[儒教|儒学]]や[[医学]]等を学んだ。後に国学を専攻し、[[1867年]]（[[慶応]]3年）に薩摩に戻った。[[明治維新|維新]]後の[[1871年]]（明治4年）に[[神祇省]]に入省し、[[教部大録]]や[[伊勢神宮]]の[[禰宜]]となった。さらにその後、[[教導職|大教正]]や[[神道事務局]]の副管長も務める。
[[1882年]]（明治15年）には管長に昇格した事により独立し、禰宜を辞職する。同時期に[[常世長胤]]や[[出雲派]]の[[千家尊福]]を批判する等、一躍有名となった。
== 主な著作物 == === 主著 === * 『校訂日本紀』 * 『校訂古事記』 * 『神徳論』 * 『(神宮）祭神略記』
=== その他の著書 === * 『三則演義』 * 『梅の屋文集』 * 『校訂古語拾遺』 * 『(神宮）祭神提要』 * 『語書』
== 出典 == * デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus（[[講談社]])『[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 田中頼庸]』- [[コトバンク]] * 朝日日本歴史人物事典（[[朝日新聞]]出版)『[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 田中頼庸]』- [[コトバンク]] * [[ブリタニカ百科事典|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典]] 小項目事典（ブリタニカ・ジャパン)『[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 田中頼庸]』- [[コトバンク]] * 美術人名事典（[[思文閣出版|思文閣]]）『[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 田中頼庸]』- [[コトバンク]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://inakaseikatsu.blogspot.com/2018/03/blog-post_15.html 田中頼庸と幕末の国学―なぜ鹿児島には神代三陵が全てあるのか？（その8）- 南薩日乗]

revision.text: #redirect[[en:Yoritsune Tanaka]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

revision.comment: Removed redirect to [[en:Yoritsune Tanaka]]
revision.text:
'''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[1836]] ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 7) - April 10, [[1897]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家}} scholar, {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} researcher, and samurai of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}} during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}}, Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門}} while studying at {{ill|Kagoshima Castle|ja|鹿児島城}}. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革}} initiated by {{ill|Shimazu Hisamitsu|ja|島津久光}}. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in {{ill|1867|ja|1867年}} (Keiō 3). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, he joined the {{ill|Jingi-kan|ja|神祇省}} (Department of Shinto Affairs) in {{ill|1871|ja|1871年}} (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録}} and later as a priest of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}.
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正}} and vice-director of the {{ill|Shinto Administration Bureau|ja|神道事務局}}. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤}} and {{ill|Senke Sonpuku|ja|千家尊福}} of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派}}. His grave is located at {{ill|Aoyama Cemetery|ja|青山霊園}} (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)祭神略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)祭神提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)

'''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[1836]] ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 7) - April 10, [[1897]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家}} scholar, {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} researcher, and samurai of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}} during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}}, Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門}} while studying at {{ill|Kagoshima Castle|ja|鹿児島城}}. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革}} initiated by {{ill|Shimazu Hisamitsu|ja|島津久光}}. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in {{ill|1867|ja|1867年}} (Keiō 3). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, he joined the {{ill|Jingi-kan|ja|神祇省}} (Department of Shinto Affairs) in {{ill|1871|ja|1871年}} (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録}} and later as a priest of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}.
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正}} and vice-director of the {{ill|Shinto Administration Bureau|ja|神道事務局}}. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤}} and {{ill|Senke Sonpuku|ja|千家尊福}} of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派}}. His grave is located at {{ill|Aoyama Cemetery|ja|青山霊園}} (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)祭神略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)祭神提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)
== References == * Digital Edition: {{ill|Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja|日本人名大辞典+Plus}} (Kodansha) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}} * {{ill|Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja|朝日日本歴史人物事典}} (Asahi Shimbun Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}} * {{ill|Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典}} (Britannica Japan) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}} * {{ill|Art Dictionary|ja|美術人名事典}} (Shibunkaku Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}}
== External Links == * [http://inakaseikatsu.blogspot.com/2018/03/blog-post_15.html Tanaka Yoritsune and Kokugaku of the Bakumatsu Period – Why Are All the Three Sacred Imperial Tombs of Mythology in Kagoshima? (Part 8) – Nansatsu Nichijō]

'''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[1836]] ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 7) - April 10, [[1897]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家}} scholar, {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} researcher, and samurai of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}} during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}}, Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門}} while studying at {{ill|Kagoshima Castle|ja|鹿児島城}}. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革}} initiated by {{ill|Shimazu Hisamitsu|ja|島津久光}}. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in {{ill|1867|ja|1867年}} (Keiō 3). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, he joined the {{ill|Jingi-kan|ja|神祇省}} (Department of Shinto Affairs) in {{ill|1871|ja|1871年}} (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録}} and later as a priest of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}.
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正}} and vice-director of the {{ill|Shinto Administration Bureau|ja|神道事務局}}. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤}} and {{ill|Senke Sonpuku|ja|千家尊福}} of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派}}. His grave is located at {{ill|Aoyama Cemetery|ja|青山霊園}} (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)祭神略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)祭神提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)
== References == * Digital Edition: {{ill|Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja|日本人名大辞典+Plus}} (Kodansha) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}} * {{ill|Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja|朝日日本歴史人物事典}} (Asahi Shimbun Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}} * {{ill|Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典}} (Britannica Japan) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}} * {{ill|Art Dictionary|ja|美術人名事典}} (Shibunkaku Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}}
== External Links == * [http://inakaseikatsu.blogspot.com/2018/03/blog-post_15.html Tanaka Yoritsune and Kokugaku of the Bakumatsu Period – Why Are All the Three Sacred Imperial Tombs of Mythology in Kagoshima? (Part 8) – Nansatsu Nichijō]
{{ill|Chief Priest of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮大宮司}} (1874–1882) {{Normdaten}} [[Category:19th-century kokugaku scholars]] [[Category:Meiji period priests]] [[Category:Priests of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ministry of Religious Education officials]] [[Category:Japanese bureaucrats (1868–1949)]] [[Category:Shinto-related people]] [[Category:People from Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Satsuma Domain samurai]] [[Category:1836 births]] [[Category:1897 deaths]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yoritsune Tanaka}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[ja:田中頼庸]]

'''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[1836]] ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 7) - April 10, [[1897]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家}} scholar, {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} researcher, and samurai of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}} during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}}, Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門}} while studying at {{ill|Kagoshima Castle|ja|鹿児島城}}. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革}} initiated by {{ill|Shimazu Hisamitsu|ja|島津久光}}. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in {{ill|1867|ja|1867年}} (Keiō 3). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, he joined the {{ill|Jingi-kan|ja|神祇省}} (Department of Shinto Affairs) in {{ill|1871|ja|1871年}} (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録}} and later as a priest of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}.
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正}} and vice-director of the {{ill|Shinto Administration Bureau|ja|神道事務局}}. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤}} and {{ill|Senke Sonpuku|ja|千家尊福}} of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派}}. His grave is located at {{ill|Aoyama Cemetery|ja|青山霊園}} (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)祭神略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)祭神提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)

revision.text:
'''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[1836]] ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 7) - April 10, [[1897]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家}} scholar, {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} researcher, and samurai of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}} during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}}, Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門}} while studying at {{ill|Kagoshima Castle|ja|鹿児島城}}. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革}} initiated by {{ill|Shimazu Hisamitsu|ja|島津久光}}. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in {{ill|1867|ja|1867年}} (Keiō 3). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, he joined the {{ill|Jingi-kan|ja|神祇省}} (Department of Shinto Affairs) in {{ill|1871|ja|1871年}} (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録}} and later as a priest of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}.
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正}} and vice-director of the {{ill|Shinto Administration Bureau|ja|神道事務局}}. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤}} and {{ill|Senke Sonpuku|ja|千家尊福}} of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派}}. His grave is located at {{ill|Aoyama Cemetery|ja|青山霊園}} (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)祭神略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)祭神提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)
== References == * Digital Edition: {{ill|Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja|日本人名大辞典+Plus}} (Kodansha) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}} * {{ill|Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja|朝日日本歴史人物事典}} (Asahi Shimbun Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}} * {{ill|Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典}} (Britannica Japan) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}} * {{ill|Art Dictionary|ja|美術人名事典}} (Shibunkaku Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}}
== External Links == * [http://inakaseikatsu.blogspot.com/2018/03/blog-post_15.html Tanaka Yoritsune and Kokugaku of the Bakumatsu Period – Why Are All the Three Sacred Imperial Tombs of Mythology in Kagoshima? (Part 8) – Nansatsu Nichijō]

'''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[1836]] ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 7) - April 10, [[1897]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家}} scholar, {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} researcher, and samurai of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}} during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}}, Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門}} while studying at {{ill|Kagoshima Castle|ja|鹿児島城}}. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革}} initiated by {{ill|Shimazu Hisamitsu|ja|島津久光}}. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in {{ill|1867|ja|1867年}} (Keiō 3). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, he joined the {{ill|Jingi-kan|ja|神祇省}} (Department of Shinto Affairs) in {{ill|1871|ja|1871年}} (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録}} and later as a priest of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}.
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正}} and vice-director of the {{ill|Shinto Administration Bureau|ja|神道事務局}}. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤}} and {{ill|Senke Sonpuku|ja|千家尊福}} of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派}}. His grave is located at {{ill|Aoyama Cemetery|ja|青山霊園}} (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)祭神略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)祭神提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)
== References == * Digital Edition: {{ill|Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja|日本人名大辞典+Plus}} (Kodansha) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}} * {{ill|Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja|朝日日本歴史人物事典}} (Asahi Shimbun Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}} * {{ill|Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典}} (Britannica Japan) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}} * {{ill|Art Dictionary|ja|美術人名事典}} (Shibunkaku Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}}
== External Links == * [http://inakaseikatsu.blogspot.com/2018/03/blog-post_15.html Tanaka Yoritsune and Kokugaku of the Bakumatsu Period – Why Are All the Three Sacred Imperial Tombs of Mythology in Kagoshima? (Part 8) – Nansatsu Nichijō]
{{ill|Chief Priest of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮大宮司}} (1874–1882) {{Normdaten}} [[Category:19th-century kokugaku scholars]] [[Category:Meiji period priests]] [[Category:Priests of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ministry of Religious Education officials]] [[Category:Japanese bureaucrats (1868–1949)]] [[Category:Shinto-related people]] [[Category:People from Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Satsuma Domain samurai]] [[Category:1836 births]] [[Category:1897 deaths]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTYoritsune Tanaka}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yoritsune Tanaka}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:田中頼庸]]

During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤}} and {{ill|Senke Sonpuku|ja|千家尊福}} of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派}}. '''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[1836]] ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 7) - April 10, [[1897]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家}} scholar, {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} researcher, and samurai of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}} during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}}, Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門}} while studying at {{ill|Kagoshima Castle|ja|鹿児島城}}. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革}} initiated by {{ill|Shimazu Hisamitsu|ja|島津久光}}. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in {{ill|1867|ja|1867年}} (Keiō 3). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, he joined the {{ill|Jingi-kan|ja|神祇省}} (Department of Shinto Affairs) in {{ill|1871|ja|1871年}} (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録}} and later as a priest of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}.
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正}} and vice-director of the {{ill|Shinto Administration Bureau|ja|神道事務局}}. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤}} and {{ill|Senke Sonpuku|ja|千家尊福}} of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派}}. His grave is located at {{ill|Aoyama Cemetery|ja|青山霊園}} (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)祭神略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)祭神提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)

'''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[1836]] ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 7) - April 10, [[1897]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家}} scholar, {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} researcher, and samurai of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}} during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}}, Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門}} while studying at {{ill|Kagoshima Castle|ja|鹿児島城}}. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革}} initiated by {{ill|Shimazu Hisamitsu|ja|島津久光}}. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in {{ill|1867|ja|1867年}} (Keiō 3). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, he joined the {{ill|Jingi-kan|ja|神祇省}} (Department of Shinto Affairs) in {{ill|1871|ja|1871年}} (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録}} and later as a priest of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}.
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正}} and vice-director of the {{ill|Shinto Administration Bureau|ja|神道事務局}}. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤}} and {{ill|Senke Sonpuku|ja|千家尊福}} of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派}}. His grave is located at {{ill|Aoyama Cemetery|ja|青山霊園}} (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)祭神略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)祭神提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)

revision.text:
'''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[1836]] ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 7) - April 10, [[1897]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家}} scholar, {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} researcher, and samurai of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}} during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}}, Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門}} while studying at {{ill|Kagoshima Castle|ja|鹿児島城}}. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革}} initiated by {{ill|Shimazu Hisamitsu|ja|島津久光}}. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in {{ill|1867|ja|1867年}} (Keiō 3). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, he joined the {{ill|Jingi-kan|ja|神祇省}} (Department of Shinto Affairs) in {{ill|1871|ja|1871年}} (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録}} and later as a priest of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}.
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正}} and vice-director of the {{ill|Shinto Administration Bureau|ja|神道事務局}}. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤}} and {{ill|Senke Sonpuku|ja|千家尊福}} of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派}}. His grave is located at {{ill|Aoyama Cemetery|ja|青山霊園}} (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)祭神略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)祭神提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)

== External Links == * [http://inakaseikatsu.blogspot.com/2018/03/blog-post_15.html Tanaka Yoritsune and Kokugaku of the Bakumatsu Period – Why Are All the Three Sacred Imperial Tombs of Mythology in Kagoshima? (Part 8) – Nansatsu Nichijō]

'''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[1836]] ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 7) - April 10, [[1897]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家}} scholar, {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} researcher, and samurai of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}} during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}}, Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門}} while studying at {{ill|Kagoshima Castle|ja|鹿児島城}}. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革}} initiated by {{ill|Shimazu Hisamitsu|ja|島津久光}}. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in {{ill|1867|ja|1867年}} (Keiō 3). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, he joined the {{ill|Jingi-kan|ja|神祇省}} (Department of Shinto Affairs) in {{ill|1871|ja|1871年}} (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録}} and later as a priest of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}.
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正}} and vice-director of the {{ill|Shinto Administration Bureau|ja|神道事務局}}. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤}} and {{ill|Senke Sonpuku|ja|千家尊福}} of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派}}. His grave is located at {{ill|Aoyama Cemetery|ja|青山霊園}} (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)祭神略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)祭神提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)

== External Links == * [http://inakaseikatsu.blogspot.com/2018/03/blog-post_15.html Tanaka Yoritsune and Kokugaku of the Bakumatsu Period – Why Are All the Three Sacred Imperial Tombs of Mythology in Kagoshima? (Part 8) – Nansatsu Nichijō]

revision.text:
'''田中 頼庸'''（たなか よりつね、[[1836年]]（[[天保]]7年）[[5月]] - [[1897年]]（[[明治]]30年）[[4月10日]]）は、[[幕末]]から明治時代の[[神道|神道家]]、[[国学|国学者]]、[[薩摩藩]]士である。通称は藤八。号は雲岫又は梅屋。
== 経歴・人物 == [[大山綱良]]を叔父に持つ薩摩藩の一家に生まれる。幼年の頃に薩摩藩士となり、[[鹿児島城]]で[[重野安繹]]、[[今藤新左衛門]]らと共に[[文学]]の秀才と名付けられた。後に[[高崎正風]]の従兄弟である[[高崎五六]]と親交を持った事で、正風は頼庸の父である綱良が属した若手の薩摩藩の組である「[[精忠組|誠忠組]]」に入ったが、頼庸は辞退する。
その後、[[島津久光]]が執行した[[文久の改革]]により活動の場を[[京都]]に移し、[[儒教|儒学]]や[[医学]]等を学んだ。後に国学を専攻し、[[1867年]]（[[慶応]]3年）に薩摩に戻った。[[明治維新|維新]]後の[[1871年]]（明治4年）に[[神祇省]]に入省し、[[教部大録]]や[[伊勢神宮]]の[[禰宜]]となった。
さらにその後、[[教導職|大教正]]や[[神道事務局]]の副管長も務める。
[[1882年]]（明治15年）には管長に昇格した事により独立し、禰宜を辞職する。同時期に[[常世長胤]]や[[出雲派]]の[[千家尊福]]を批判する等、一躍有名となった。墓所は[[青山霊園]](1ロ17-14)
== 主な著作物 == === 主著 === * 『校訂日本紀』 * 『校訂古事記』 * 『神徳論』 * 『(神宮）祭神略記』
=== その他の著書 === * 『三則演義』 * 『梅の屋文集』 * 『校訂古語拾遺』 * 『(神宮）祭神提要』 * 『語書』
== 出典 == * デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus（[[講談社]]）『[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 田中頼庸]』- [[コトバンク]] * 朝日日本歴史人物事典（[[朝日新聞]]出版）『[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 田中頼庸]』- [[コトバンク]] * [[ブリタニカ百科事典|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典]] 小項目事典（ブリタニカ・ジャパン）『[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 田中頼庸]』- [[コトバンク]] * 美術人名事典（[[思文閣出版|思文閣]]）『[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 田中頼庸]』- [[コトバンク]]
== 外部リンク == * [https://inakaseikatsu.blogspot.com/2018/03/blog-post_15.html 田中頼庸と幕末の国学―なぜ鹿児島には神代三陵が全てあるのか？（その8）- 南薩日乗]
{{伊勢神宮大宮司|1874年 - 1882年}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たなか よりつね}} [[Category:19世紀の国学者]] [[Category:明治時代の神職]] [[Category:伊勢神宮の祠官]] [[Category:教部省の人物]] [[Category:日本の官僚 (1868-1949)]] [[Category:神道に関連する人物]] [[Category:鹿児島県出身の人物]] [[Category:幕末薩摩藩の人物]] [[Category:1836年生]] [[Category:1897年没]]

revision.text:
'''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[1836]] ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 7) - April 10, [[1897]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家}} scholar, {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} researcher, and samurai of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}} during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}}, Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門}} while studying at {{ill|Kagoshima Castle|ja|鹿児島城}}. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革}} initiated by {{ill|Shimazu Hisamitsu|ja|島津久光}}. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in {{ill|1867|ja|1867年}} (Keiō 3). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, he joined the {{ill|Jingi-kan|ja|神祇省}} (Department of Shinto Affairs) in {{ill|1871|ja|1871年}} (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録}} and later as a priest of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}.
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正}} and vice-director of the {{ill|Shinto Administration Bureau|ja|神道事務局}}. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤}} and {{ill|Senke Sonpuku|ja|千家尊福}} of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派}}. His grave is located at {{ill|Aoyama Cemetery|ja|青山霊園}} (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)deity略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)
== References == * Digital Edition: {{ill|Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja|日本人名大辞典+Plus}} (Kodansha) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}} * {{ill|Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja|朝日日本歴史人物事典}} (Asahi Shimbun Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}} * {{ill|Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典}} (Britannica Japan) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}} * {{ill|Art Dictionary|ja|美術人名事典}} (Shibunkaku Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}}
== External Links == * [http://inakaseikatsu.blogspot.com/2018/03/blog-post_15.html Tanaka Yoritsune and Kokugaku of the Bakumatsu Period – Why Are All the Three Sacred Imperial Tombs of Mythology in Kagoshima? (Part 8) – Nansatsu Nichijō]

'''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[1836]] ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 7) - April 10, [[1897]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家}} scholar, {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} researcher, and samurai of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}} during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}}, Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門}} while studying at {{ill|Kagoshima Castle|ja|鹿児島城}}. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革}} initiated by {{ill|Shimazu Hisamitsu|ja|島津久光}}. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in {{ill|1867|ja|1867年}} (Keiō 3). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, he joined the {{ill|Jingi-kan|ja|神祇省}} (Department of Shinto Affairs) in {{ill|1871|ja|1871年}} (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録}} and later as a priest of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}.
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正}} and vice-director of the {{ill|Shinto Administration Bureau|ja|神道事務局}}. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤}} and {{ill|Senke Sonpuku|ja|千家尊福}} of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派}}. His grave is located at {{ill|Aoyama Cemetery|ja|青山霊園}} (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)deity略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)

In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤}} and {{ill|Senke Sonpuku|ja|千家尊福}} of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派}}. '''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[1836]] ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 7) - April 10, [[1897]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家}} scholar, {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} researcher, and samurai of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}} during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}}, Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門}} while studying at {{ill|Kagoshima Castle|ja|鹿児島城}}. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革}} initiated by {{ill|Shimazu Hisamitsu|ja|島津久光}}. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in {{ill|1867|ja|1867年}} (Keiō 3). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, he joined the {{ill|Jingi-kan|ja|神祇省}} (Department of Shinto Affairs) in {{ill|1871|ja|1871年}} (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録}} and later as a priest of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}.
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正}} and vice-director of the {{ill|Shinto Administration Bureau|ja|神道事務局}}. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤}} and {{ill|Senke Sonpuku|ja|千家尊福}} of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派}}. His grave is located at {{ill|Aoyama Cemetery|ja|青山霊園}} (1-ro-17-14).

=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)

revision.text:
'''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[1836]] ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 7) - April 10, [[1897]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家}} scholar, {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} researcher, and samurai of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}} during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}}, Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門}} while studying at {{ill|Kagoshima Castle|ja|鹿児島城}}. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革}} initiated by {{ill|Shimazu Hisamitsu|ja|島津久光}}. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in {{ill|1867|ja|1867年}} (Keiō 3). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, he joined the {{ill|Jingi-kan|ja|神祇省}} (Department of Shinto Affairs) in {{ill|1871|ja|1871年}} (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録}} and later as a priest of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}.
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正}} and vice-director of the {{ill|Shinto Administration Bureau|ja|神道事務局}}. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤}} and {{ill|Senke Sonpuku|ja|千家尊福}} of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派}}. His grave is located at {{ill|Aoyama Cemetery|ja|青山霊園}} (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)deity略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)
== References == * Digital Edition: {{ill|Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja|日本人名大辞典+Plus}} (Kodansha) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}} * {{ill|Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja|朝日日本歴史人物事典}} (Asahi Shimbun Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}} * {{ill|Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典}} (Britannica Japan) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}} * {{ill|Art Dictionary|ja|美術人名事典}} (Shibunkaku Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}}
== External Links == * [http://inakaseikatsu.blogspot.com/2018/03/blog-post_15.html Tanaka Yoritsune and Kokugaku of the Bakumatsu Period – Why Are All the Three Sacred Imperial Tombs of Mythology in Kagoshima? (Part 8) – Nansatsu Nichijō]

Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正}} and vice-director of the {{ill|Shinto Administration Bureau|ja|神道事務局}}. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤}} and {{ill|Senke Sonpuku|ja|千家尊福}} of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派}}. His grave is located at {{ill|Aoyama Cemetery|ja|青山霊園}} (1-ro-17-14).

'''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[1836]] ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 7) - April 10, [[1897]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家}} scholar, {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} researcher, and samurai of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}} during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}}, Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門}} while studying at {{ill|Kagoshima Castle|ja|鹿児島城}}. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革}} initiated by {{ill|Shimazu Hisamitsu|ja|島津久光}}. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in {{ill|1867|ja|1867年}} (Keiō 3). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, he joined the {{ill|Jingi-kan|ja|神祇省}} (Department of Shinto Affairs) in {{ill|1871|ja|1871年}} (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録}} and later as a priest of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}.
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正}} and vice-director of the {{ill|Shinto Administration Bureau|ja|神道事務局}}. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤}} and {{ill|Senke Sonpuku|ja|千家尊福}} of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派}}. His grave is located at {{ill|Aoyama Cemetery|ja|青山霊園}} (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)deity略記』)

'''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[1836]] ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 7) - April 10, [[1897]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家}} scholar, {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} researcher, and samurai of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}} during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}}, Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門}} while studying at {{ill|Kagoshima Castle|ja|鹿児島城}}. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革}} initiated by {{ill|Shimazu Hisamitsu|ja|島津久光}}. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in {{ill|1867|ja|1867年}} (Keiō 3). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, he joined the {{ill|Jingi-kan|ja|神祇省}} (Department of Shinto Affairs) in {{ill|1871|ja|1871年}} (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録}} and later as a priest of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}.
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正}} and vice-director of the {{ill|Shinto Administration Bureau|ja|神道事務局}}. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤}} and {{ill|Senke Sonpuku|ja|千家尊福}} of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派}}. His grave is located at {{ill|Aoyama Cemetery|ja|青山霊園}} (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)deity略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)
== References == * Digital Edition: {{ill|Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja|日本人名大辞典+Plus}} (Kodansha) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}} * {{ill|Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja|朝日日本歴史人物事典}} (Asahi Shimbun Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}} * {{ill|Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典}} (Britannica Japan) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}} * {{ill|Art Dictionary|ja|美術人名事典}} (Shibunkaku Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}}
== External Links == * [http://inakaseikatsu.blogspot.com/2018/03/blog-post_15.html Tanaka Yoritsune and Kokugaku of the Bakumatsu Period – Why Are All the Three Sacred Imperial Tombs of Mythology in Kagoshima? (Part 8) – Nansatsu Nichijō]
{{ill|Chief Priest of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮大宮司}} (1874–1882) {{Normdaten}} [[Category:19th-century kokugaku scholars]] [[Category:Meiji period priests]] [[Category:Priests of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ministry of Religious Education officials]] [[Category:Japanese bureaucrats (1868–1949)]] [[Category:Shinto-related people]] [[Category:People from Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Satsuma Domain samurai]] [[Category:1836 births]] [[Category:1897 deaths]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yoritsune Tanaka}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Yoritsune Tanaka-->[[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoritsune Tanaka]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yoritsune Tanaka}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Yoritsune Tanaka]]{{citation needed|Yoritsune Tanaka}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yoritsune Tanaka]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Yoritsune Tanaka]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Yoritsune Tanaka]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoritsune Tanaka]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoritsune Tanaka]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoritsune Tanaka]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoritsune Tanaka]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Yoritsune Tanaka]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoritsune Tanaka]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoritsune Tanaka]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoritsune Tanaka]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:田中頼庸]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoritsune Tanaka]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Yoritsune Tanaka]]

revision.text:
'''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[1836]] ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 7) - April 10, [[1897]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家}} scholar, {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} researcher, and samurai of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}} during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}}, Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門}} while studying at {{ill|Kagoshima Castle|ja|鹿児島城}}. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革}} initiated by {{ill|Shimazu Hisamitsu|ja|島津久光}}. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in {{ill|1867|ja|1867年}} (Keiō 3). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, he joined the {{ill|Jingi-kan|ja|神祇省}} (Department of Shinto Affairs) in {{ill|1871|ja|1871年}} (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録}} and later as a priest of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}.
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正}} and vice-director of the {{ill|Shinto Administration Bureau|ja|神道事務局}}. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤}} and {{ill|Senke Sonpuku|ja|千家尊福}} of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派}}. His grave is located at {{ill|Aoyama Cemetery|ja|青山霊園}} (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)deity略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)
== References == * Digital Edition: {{ill|Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja|日本人名大辞典+Plus}} (Kodansha) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}} * {{ill|Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja|朝日日本歴史人物事典}} (Asahi Shimbun Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}} * {{ill|Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典}} (Britannica Japan) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}} * {{ill|Art Dictionary|ja|美術人名事典}} (Shibunkaku Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}}
== External Links == * [http://inakaseikatsu.blogspot.com/2018/03/blog-post_15.html Tanaka Yoritsune and Kokugaku of the Bakumatsu Period – Why Are All the Three Sacred Imperial Tombs of Mythology in Kagoshima? (Part 8) – Nansatsu Nichijō]

During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤}} and {{ill|Senke Sonpuku|ja|千家尊福}} of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派}}. His grave is located at {{ill|Aoyama Cemetery|ja|青山霊園}} (1-ro-17-14).

=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)

'''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[1836]] ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 7) - April 10, [[1897]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家}} scholar, {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} researcher, and samurai of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}} during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}}, Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門}} while studying at {{ill|Kagoshima Castle|ja|鹿児島城}}. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革}} initiated by {{ill|Shimazu Hisamitsu|ja|島津久光}}. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in {{ill|1867|ja|1867年}} (Keiō 3). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, he joined the {{ill|Jingi-kan|ja|神祇省}} (Department of Shinto Affairs) in {{ill|1871|ja|1871年}} (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録}} and later as a priest of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}.
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正}} and vice-director of the {{ill|Shinto Administration Bureau|ja|神道事務局}}. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤}} and {{ill|Senke Sonpuku|ja|千家尊福}} of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派}}. His grave is located at {{ill|Aoyama Cemetery|ja|青山霊園}} (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)deity略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)
== References == * Digital Edition: {{ill|Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja|日本人名大辞典+Plus}} (Kodansha) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}} * {{ill|Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja|朝日日本歴史人物事典}} (Asahi Shimbun Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}} * {{ill|Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典}} (Britannica Japan) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}} * {{ill|Art Dictionary|ja|美術人名事典}} (Shibunkaku Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}}
== External Links == * [http://inakaseikatsu.blogspot.com/2018/03/blog-post_15.html Tanaka Yoritsune and Kokugaku of the Bakumatsu Period – Why Are All the Three Sacred Imperial Tombs of Mythology in Kagoshima? (Part 8) – Nansatsu Nichijō]

His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩}}, Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門}} while studying at {{ill|Kagoshima Castle|ja|鹿児島城}}. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革}} initiated by {{ill|Shimazu Hisamitsu|ja|島津久光}}. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in {{ill|1867|ja|1867年}} (Keiō 3). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, he joined the {{ill|Jingi-kan|ja|神祇省}} (Department of Shinto Affairs) in {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year }} (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録}} and later as a priest of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}.
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正}} and vice-director of the {{ill|Shinto Administration Bureau|ja|神道事務局}}. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15th year }}, he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤}} and {{ill|Senke Sonpuku|ja|千家尊福}} of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派}}. His grave is located at {{ill|Aoyama Cemetery|ja|青山霊園}} (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)deity略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)

His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.

From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門}} while studying at {{ill|Kagoshima Castle|ja|鹿児島城}}. Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正}} and vice-director of the {{ill|Shinto Administration Bureau|ja|神道事務局}}. revision.text:
'''田中 頼庸'''（たなか よりつね、[[1836年]]（[[天保]]7年）[[5月]] - [[1897年]]（[[明治]]30年）[[4月10日]]）は、[[幕末]]から明治時代の[[神道|神道家]]、[[国学|国学者]]、[[薩摩藩]]士である。通称は藤八。号は雲岫又は梅屋。
== 経歴・人物 == [[大山綱良]]を叔父に持つ薩摩藩の一家に生まれる。幼年の頃に薩摩藩士となり、[[鹿児島城]]で[[重野安繹]]、[[今藤新左衛門]]らと共に[[文学]]の秀才と名付けられた。後に[[高崎正風]]の従兄弟である[[高崎五六]]と親交を持った事で、正風は頼庸の父である綱良が属した若手の薩摩藩の組である「[[精忠組|誠忠組]]」に入ったが、頼庸は辞退する。
その後、[[島津久光]]が執行した[[文久の改革]]により活動の場を[[京都]]に移し、[[儒教|儒学]]や[[医学]]等を学んだ。後に国学を専攻し、[[1867年]]（[[慶応]]3年）に薩摩に戻った。[[明治維新|維新]]後の[[1871年]]（明治4年）に[[神祇省]]に入省し、[[教部大録]]や[[伊勢神宮]]の[[禰宜]]となった。
さらにその後、[[教導職|大教正]]や[[神道事務局]]の副管長も務める。
[[1882年]]（明治15年）には管長に昇格した事により独立し、禰宜を辞職する。同時期に[[常世長胤]]や[[出雲派]]の[[千家尊福]]を批判する等、一躍有名となった。墓所は[[青山霊園]](1ロ17-14)
== 主な著作物 == === 主著 === * 『校訂日本紀』 * 『校訂古事記』 * 『神徳論』 * 『(神宮）祭神略記』
=== その他の著書 === * 『三則演義』 * 『梅の屋文集』 * 『校訂古語拾遺』 * 『(神宮）祭神提要』 * 『語書』
== 出典 == * デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus（[[講談社]]）『[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 田中頼庸]』- [[コトバンク]] * 朝日日本歴史人物事典（[[朝日新聞]]出版）『[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 田中頼庸]』- [[コトバンク]] * [[ブリタニカ百科事典|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典]] 小項目事典（ブリタニカ・ジャパン）『[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 田中頼庸]』- [[コトバンク]] * 美術人名事典（[[思文閣出版|思文閣]]）『[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 田中頼庸]』- [[コトバンク]]
== 外部リンク == * [https://inakaseikatsu.blogspot.com/2018/03/blog-post_15.html 田中頼庸と幕末の国学―なぜ鹿児島には神代三陵が全てあるのか？（その8）- 南薩日乗]

revision.text:
'''田中 頼庸'''（たなか よりつね、[[1836年]]（[[天保]]7年）[[5月]] - [[1897年]]（[[明治]]30年）[[4月10日]]）は、[[幕末]]から明治時代の[[神道|神道家]]、[[国学|国学者]]、[[薩摩藩]]士である。通称は藤八。号は雲岫又は梅屋。
== 経歴・人物 == [[大山綱良]]を叔父に持つ薩摩藩の一家に生まれる。幼年の頃に薩摩藩士となり、[[鹿児島城]]で[[重野安繹]]、[[今藤新左衛門]]らと共に[[文学]]の秀才と名付けられた。後に[[高崎正風]]の従兄弟である[[高崎五六]]と親交を持った事で、正風は頼庸の父である綱良が属した若手の薩摩藩の組である「[[精忠組|誠忠組]]」に入ったが、頼庸は辞退する。
その後、[[島津久光]]が執行した[[文久の改革]]により活動の場を[[京都]]に移し、[[儒教|儒学]]や[[医学]]等を学んだ。後に国学を専攻し、[[1867年]]（[[慶応]]3年）に薩摩に戻った。[[明治維新|維新]]後の[[1871年]]（明治4年）に[[神祇省]]に入省し、[[教部大録]]や[[伊勢神宮]]の[[禰宜]]となった。
さらにその後、[[教導職|大教正]]や[[神道事務局]]の副管長も務める。
[[1882年]]（明治15年）には管長に昇格した事により独立し、禰宜を辞職する。同時期に[[常世長胤]]や[[出雲派]]の[[千家尊福]]を批判する等、一躍有名となった。墓所は[[青山霊園]](1ロ17-14)
== 主な著作物 == === 主著 === * 『校訂日本紀』 * 『校訂古事記』 * 『神徳論』 * 『(神宮）祭神略記』
=== その他の著書 === * 『三則演義』 * 『梅の屋文集』 * 『校訂古語拾遺』 * 『(神宮）祭神提要』 * 『語書』
== 出典 == * デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus（[[講談社]]）『[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 田中頼庸]』- [[コトバンク]] * 朝日日本歴史人物事典（[[朝日新聞]]出版）『[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 田中頼庸]』- [[コトバンク]] * [[ブリタニカ百科事典|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典]] 小項目事典（ブリタニカ・ジャパン）『[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 田中頼庸]』- [[コトバンク]] * 美術人名事典（[[思文閣出版|思文閣]]）『[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 田中頼庸]』- [[コトバンク]]
== 外部リンク == * [https://inakaseikatsu.blogspot.com/2018/03/blog-post_15.html 田中頼庸と幕末の国学―なぜ鹿児島には神代三陵が全てあるのか？（その8）- 南薩日乗]
{{伊勢神宮大宮司|1874年 - 1882年}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たなか よりつね}} [[Category:19世紀の国学者]] [[Category:明治時代の神職]] [[Category:伊勢神宮の祠官]] [[Category:教部省の人物]] [[Category:日本の官僚 (1868-1949)]] [[Category:神道に関連する人物]] [[Category:鹿児島県出身の人物]] [[Category:幕末薩摩藩の人物]] [[Category:1836年生]] [[Category:1897年没]]

revision.text:
'''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[1836]] ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpō|qq=}} 7) - April 10, [[1897]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} scholar, {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kokugaku|qq=}} researcher, and samurai of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsuma Domain|qq=}} during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsuma Domain|qq=}}, Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsunayoshi|qq=}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeno Yasutsugu|qq=}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imafuji Shinzaemon|qq=}} while studying at {{ill|Kagoshima Castle|ja|鹿児島城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Castle|qq=}}. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Goroku|qq=}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Masakaze|qq=}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seichūgumi|qq=}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Reforms|qq=}} initiated by {{ill|Shimazu Hisamitsu|ja|島津久光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu Hisamitsu|qq=}}. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in {{ill|1867|ja|1867th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1867|qq=}} (Keiō 3). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, he joined the {{ill|Department of Divinities|ja|神祇省|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingi-kan|qq=}} (Department of Shinto Affairs) in {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1871|qq=}} (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōbu Daikan|qq=}} and later as a priest of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}.
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kyōsei|qq=}} and vice-director of the {{ill|Bureau of Shinto Affairs|ja|神道事務局|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Administration Bureau|qq=}}. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1882|qq=}}, he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokoyo Nagatane|qq=}} and {{ill|Senke Sonpuku|ja|千家尊福|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senke Sonpuku|qq=}} of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo School|qq=}}. His grave is located at {{ill|Aoyama Cemetery|ja|青山霊園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyama Cemetery|qq=}} (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)deity略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)
== References == * Digital Edition: {{ill|Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja|日本人名大辞典+Plus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|qq=}} (Kodansha) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotobank|qq=}} * {{ill|Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja|朝日日本歴史人物事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|qq=}} (Asahi Shimbun Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotobank|qq=}} * {{ill|Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|qq=}} (Britannica Japan) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotobank|qq=}} * {{ill|Art Dictionary|ja|美術人名事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Art Dictionary|qq=}} (Shibunkaku Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotobank|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://inakaseikatsu.blogspot.com/2018/03/blog-post_15.html Tanaka Yoritsune and Kokugaku of the Bakumatsu Period – Why Are All the Three Sacred Imperial Tombs of Mythology in Kagoshima? (Part 8) – Nansatsu Nichijō]
{{ill|Chief Priest of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮大宮司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief Priest of Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}} (1874–1882) {{Normdaten}}
{{translated page|ja|田中頼庸|version=103828780|comment=Imported full ja history}}
