'''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[1836]] ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpō|qq=}} 7) - April 10, [[1897]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} scholar, {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kokugaku|qq=}} researcher, and samurai of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsuma Domain|qq=}} during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|ja|薩摩藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsuma Domain|qq=}}, Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsunayoshi|qq=}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeno Yasutsugu|qq=}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imafuji Shinzaemon|qq=}} while studying at {{ill|Kagoshima Castle|ja|鹿児島城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Castle|qq=}}. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Goroku|qq=}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Masakaze|qq=}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seichūgumi|qq=}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Reforms|qq=}} initiated by {{ill|Shimazu Hisamitsu|ja|島津久光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu Hisamitsu|qq=}}. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in {{ill|1867|ja|1867th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1867|qq=}} (Keiō 3). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, he joined the {{ill|Department of Divinities|ja|神祇省|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingi-kan|qq=}} (Department of Shinto Affairs) in {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1871|qq=}} (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōbu Daikan|qq=}} and later as a priest of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}.
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kyōsei|qq=}} and vice-director of the {{ill|Bureau of Shinto Affairs|ja|神道事務局|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Administration Bureau|qq=}}. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1882|qq=}}, he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokoyo Nagatane|qq=}} and {{ill|Senke Sonpuku|ja|千家尊福|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senke Sonpuku|qq=}} of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo School|qq=}}. His grave is located at {{ill|Aoyama Cemetery|ja|青山霊園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyama Cemetery|qq=}} (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)deity略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)

[[sn:Yoritsune Tanaka]] [[en:Yoritsune Tanaka]] [[ja:田中頼庸]]
<!--Yoritsune Tanaka-->

revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
'''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[1836]] ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 7) - April 10, [[1897]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} scholar, [[:en:Kokugaku|kokugaku]] researcher, and samurai of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]] during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]], Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsunayoshi|qq=}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeno Yasutsugu|qq=}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imafuji Shinzaemon|qq=}} while studying at [[:en:Kagoshima Castle|Kagoshima Castle]]. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Goroku|qq=}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Masakaze|qq=}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seichūgumi|qq=}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Reforms|qq=}} initiated by [[:en:Shimazu Hisamitsu|Shimazu Hisamitsu]]. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in [[:en:1867|1867]] (Keiō 3). After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], he joined the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] (Department of Shinto Affairs) in [[:en:1871|1871]] (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōbu Daikan|qq=}} and later as a priest of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]].
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kyōsei|qq=}} and vice-director of the [[:en:Bureau of Shinto Affairs|Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1882|qq=}}, he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokoyo Nagatane|qq=}} and [[:en:Senge Takatomi|Senke Sonpuku]] of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo School|qq=}}. His grave is located at [[:en:Aoyama Cemetery|Aoyama Cemetery]] (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)deity略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)
== References == * Digital Edition: {{ill|Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja|日本人名大辞典+Plus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|qq=}} (Kodansha) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja|朝日日本歴史人物事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|qq=}} (Asahi Shimbun Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|qq=}} (Britannica Japan) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Art Dictionary|ja|美術人名事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Art Dictionary|qq=}} (Shibunkaku Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]]
== External Links == * [http://inakaseikatsu.blogspot.com/2018/03/blog-post_15.html Tanaka Yoritsune and Kokugaku of the Bakumatsu Period – Why Are All the Three Sacred Imperial Tombs of Mythology in Kagoshima? (Part 8) – Nansatsu Nichijō]
{{ill|Chief Priest of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮大宮司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief Priest of Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}} (1874–1882) {{Normdaten}}

His grave is located at [[:en:Aoyama Cemetery|Aoyama Cemetery]] (1-ro-17-14).

'''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[1836]] ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 7) - April 10, [[1897]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} scholar, [[:en:Kokugaku|kokugaku]] researcher, and samurai of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]] during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]], Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsunayoshi|qq=}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeno Yasutsugu|qq=}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imafuji Shinzaemon|qq=}} while studying at [[:en:Kagoshima Castle|Kagoshima Castle]]. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Goroku|qq=}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Masakaze|qq=}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seichūgumi|qq=}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Reforms|qq=}} initiated by [[:en:Shimazu Hisamitsu|Shimazu Hisamitsu]]. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in [[:en:1867|1867]] (Keiō 3). After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], he joined the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] (Department of Shinto Affairs) in [[:en:1871|1871]] (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōbu Daikan|qq=}} and later as a priest of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]].
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kyōsei|qq=}} and vice-director of the [[:en:Bureau of Shinto Affairs|Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1882|qq=}}, he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokoyo Nagatane|qq=}} and [[:en:Senge Takatomi|Senke Sonpuku]] of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo School|qq=}}. His grave is located at [[:en:Aoyama Cemetery|Aoyama Cemetery]] (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)deity略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:Shintoists
revision.text:
'''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[1836]] ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 7) - April 10, [[1897]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} scholar, [[:en:Kokugaku|kokugaku]] researcher, and samurai of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]] during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]], Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsunayoshi|qq=}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeno Yasutsugu|qq=}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imafuji Shinzaemon|qq=}} while studying at [[:en:Kagoshima Castle|Kagoshima Castle]]. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Goroku|qq=}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Masakaze|qq=}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seichūgumi|qq=}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Reforms|qq=}} initiated by [[:en:Shimazu Hisamitsu|Shimazu Hisamitsu]]. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in [[:en:1867|1867]] (Keiō 3). After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], he joined the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] (Department of Shinto Affairs) in [[:en:1871|1871]] (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōbu Daikan|qq=}} and later as a priest of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]].
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kyōsei|qq=}} and vice-director of the [[:en:Bureau of Shinto Affairs|Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1882|qq=}}, he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokoyo Nagatane|qq=}} and [[:en:Senge Takatomi|Senke Sonpuku]] of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo School|qq=}}. His grave is located at [[:en:Aoyama Cemetery|Aoyama Cemetery]] (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)deity略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)
== References == * Digital Edition: {{ill|Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja|日本人名大辞典+Plus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|qq=}} (Kodansha) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja|朝日日本歴史人物事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|qq=}} (Asahi Shimbun Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|qq=}} (Britannica Japan) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Art Dictionary|ja|美術人名事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Art Dictionary|qq=}} (Shibunkaku Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]]
== External Links == * [http://inakaseikatsu.blogspot.com/2018/03/blog-post_15.html Tanaka Yoritsune and Kokugaku of the Bakumatsu Period – Why Are All the Three Sacred Imperial Tombs of Mythology in Kagoshima? (Part 8) – Nansatsu Nichijō]
{{ill|Chief Priest of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮大宮司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief Priest of Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}} (1874–1882) {{Normdaten}}

revision.text:
'''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[1836]] ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 7) - April 10, [[1897]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} scholar, [[:en:Kokugaku|kokugaku]] researcher, and samurai of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]] during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]], Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsunayoshi|qq=}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeno Yasutsugu|qq=}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imafuji Shinzaemon|qq=}} while studying at [[:en:Kagoshima Castle|Kagoshima Castle]]. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Goroku|qq=}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Masakaze|qq=}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seichūgumi|qq=}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Reforms|qq=}} initiated by [[:en:Shimazu Hisamitsu|Shimazu Hisamitsu]]. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in [[:en:1867|1867]] (Keiō 3). After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], he joined the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] (Department of Shinto Affairs) in [[:en:1871|1871]] (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōbu Daikan|qq=}} and later as a priest of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]].
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kyōsei|qq=}} and vice-director of the [[:en:Bureau of Shinto Affairs|Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1882|qq=}}, he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokoyo Nagatane|qq=}} and [[:en:Senge Takatomi|Senke Sonpuku]] of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo School|qq=}}. His grave is located at [[:en:Aoyama Cemetery|Aoyama Cemetery]] (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)deity略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)
== References == * Digital Edition: {{ill|Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja|日本人名大辞典+Plus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|qq=}} (Kodansha) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja|朝日日本歴史人物事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|qq=}} (Asahi Shimbun Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|qq=}} (Britannica Japan) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Art Dictionary|ja|美術人名事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Art Dictionary|qq=}} (Shibunkaku Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]]
== External Links == * [http://inakaseikatsu.blogspot.com/2018/03/blog-post_15.html Tanaka Yoritsune and Kokugaku of the Bakumatsu Period – Why Are All the Three Sacred Imperial Tombs of Mythology in Kagoshima? (Part 8) – Nansatsu Nichijō]
{{ill|Chief Priest of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮大宮司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief Priest of Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}} (1874–1882)

After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], he joined the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] (Department of Shinto Affairs) in [[:en:1871|1871]] (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōbu Daikan|qq=}} and later as a priest of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]].
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kyōsei|qq=}} and vice-director of the [[:en:Bureau of Shinto Affairs|Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1882|qq=}}, he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokoyo Nagatane|qq=}} and [[:en:Senge Takatomi|Senke Sonpuku]] of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo School|qq=}}. His grave is located at [[:en:Aoyama Cemetery|Aoyama Cemetery]] (1-ro-17-14).

=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)

'''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[:en:1836|1836]] ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 7) - April 10, [[1897]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} scholar, [[:en:Kokugaku|kokugaku]] researcher, and samurai of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]] during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]], Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsunayoshi|qq=}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeno Yasutsugu|qq=}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imafuji Shinzaemon|qq=}} while studying at [[:en:Kagoshima Castle|Kagoshima Castle]]. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Goroku|qq=}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Masakaze|qq=}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seichūgumi|qq=}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Reforms|qq=}} initiated by [[:en:Shimazu Hisamitsu|Shimazu Hisamitsu]]. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in [[:en:1867|1867]] (Keiō 3). After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], he joined the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] (Department of Shinto Affairs) in [[:en:1871|1871]] (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōbu Daikan|qq=}} and later as a priest of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]].
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kyōsei|qq=}} and vice-director of the [[:en:Bureau of Shinto Affairs|Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1882|qq=}}, he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokoyo Nagatane|qq=}} and [[:en:Senge Takatomi|Senke Sonpuku]] of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo School|qq=}}. His grave is located at [[:en:Aoyama Cemetery|Aoyama Cemetery]] (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)deity略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)
== References == * Digital Edition: {{ill|Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja|日本人名大辞典+Plus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|qq=}} (Kodansha) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja|朝日日本歴史人物事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|qq=}} (Asahi Shimbun Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|qq=}} (Britannica Japan) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Art Dictionary|ja|美術人名事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Art Dictionary|qq=}} (Shibunkaku Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Ministry of Religious Education officials
revision.text:
'''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[:en:1836|1836]] ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 7) - April 10, [[:en:1897|1897]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道}} scholar, [[:en:Kokugaku|kokugaku]] researcher, and samurai of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]] during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]], Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsunayoshi|qq=}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeno Yasutsugu|qq=}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imafuji Shinzaemon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} while studying at [[:en:Kagoshima Castle|Kagoshima Castle]]. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Goroku|qq=}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Masakaze|qq=}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seichūgumi|qq=}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Reforms|qq=}} initiated by [[:en:Shimazu Hisamitsu|Shimazu Hisamitsu]]. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in [[:en:1867|1867]] (Keiō 3). After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], he joined the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] (Department of Shinto Affairs) in [[:en:1871|1871]] (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōbu Daikan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later as a priest of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]].
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kyōsei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 教導職}} and vice-director of the [[:en:Bureau of Shinto Affairs|Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1882|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1882年}}, he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokoyo Nagatane|qq=}} and [[:en:Senge Takatomi|Senke Sonpuku]] of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo School|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His grave is located at [[:en:Aoyama Cemetery|Aoyama Cemetery]] (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)deity略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)
== References == * Digital Edition: {{ill|Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja|日本人名大辞典+Plus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kodansha) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja|朝日日本歴史人物事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Asahi Shimbun Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Britannica Japan) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Art Dictionary|ja|美術人名事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Art Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shibunkaku Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]]

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:伊勢神宮の祠官]] into [[Category:Shinkan of Ise Grand Shrine]] via bot
revision.text:
'''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[:en:1836|1836]] ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 7) - April 10, [[:en:1897|1897]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道}} scholar, [[:en:Kokugaku|kokugaku]] researcher, and samurai of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]] during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]], Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsunayoshi|qq=}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeno Yasutsugu|qq=}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imafuji Shinzaemon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} while studying at [[:en:Kagoshima Castle|Kagoshima Castle]]. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Goroku|qq=}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Masakaze|qq=}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seichūgumi|qq=}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Reforms|qq=}} initiated by [[:en:Shimazu Hisamitsu|Shimazu Hisamitsu]]. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in [[:en:1867|1867]] (Keiō 3). After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], he joined the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] (Department of Shinto Affairs) in [[:en:1871|1871]] (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōbu Daikan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later as a priest of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]].
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kyōsei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 教導職}} and vice-director of the [[:en:Bureau of Shinto Affairs|Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. In [[:en:1882|1882]], he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokoyo Nagatane|qq=}} and [[:en:Senge Takatomi|Senke Sonpuku]] of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo School|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His grave is located at [[:en:Aoyama Cemetery|Aoyama Cemetery]] (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)deity略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)
== References == * Digital Edition: {{ill|Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja|日本人名大辞典+Plus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kodansha) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja|朝日日本歴史人物事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Asahi Shimbun Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Britannica Japan) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Art Dictionary|ja|美術人名事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Art Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shibunkaku Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]]
== External Links == * [http://inakaseikatsu.blogspot.com/2018/03/blog-post_15.html Tanaka Yoritsune and Kokugaku of the Bakumatsu Period – Why Are All the Three Sacred Imperial Tombs of Mythology in Kagoshima? (Part 8) – Nansatsu Nichijō]

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:日本の官僚 (1868-1949)]] into [[Category:Bureaucrats of Japan (1868-1949)]] via bot
revision.text:
'''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[:en:1836|1836]] ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 7) - April 10, [[:en:1897|1897]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道}} scholar, [[:en:Kokugaku|kokugaku]] researcher, and samurai of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]] during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]], Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsunayoshi|qq=}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeno Yasutsugu|qq=}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imafuji Shinzaemon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} while studying at [[:en:Kagoshima Castle|Kagoshima Castle]]. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Goroku|qq=}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Masakaze|qq=}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seichūgumi|qq=}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Reforms|qq=}} initiated by [[:en:Shimazu Hisamitsu|Shimazu Hisamitsu]]. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in [[:en:1867|1867]] (Keiō 3). After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], he joined the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] (Department of Shinto Affairs) in [[:en:1871|1871]] (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōbu Daikan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later as a priest of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]].
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kyōsei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 教導職}} and vice-director of the [[:en:Bureau of Shinto Affairs|Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. In [[:en:1882|1882]], he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokoyo Nagatane|qq=}} and [[:en:Senge Takatomi|Senke Sonpuku]] of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo School|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His grave is located at [[:en:Aoyama Cemetery|Aoyama Cemetery]] (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)deity略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)
== References == * Digital Edition: {{ill|Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja|日本人名大辞典+Plus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kodansha) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja|朝日日本歴史人物事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Asahi Shimbun Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Britannica Japan) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Art Dictionary|ja|美術人名事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Art Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shibunkaku Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]]
== External Links == * [http://inakaseikatsu.blogspot.com/2018/03/blog-post_15.html Tanaka Yoritsune and Kokugaku of the Bakumatsu Period – Why Are All the Three Sacred Imperial Tombs of Mythology in Kagoshima? (Part 8) – Nansatsu Nichijō]

revision.comment: Bot: prepend Wikidata AFC comment via pages.txt
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q95162647]] Wikidata name: Tanaka Yoritsune [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[:en:1836|1836]] ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 7) - April 10, [[:en:1897|1897]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道}} scholar, [[:en:Kokugaku|kokugaku]] researcher, and samurai of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]] during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]], Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsunayoshi|qq=}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeno Yasutsugu|qq=}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imafuji Shinzaemon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} while studying at [[:en:Kagoshima Castle|Kagoshima Castle]]. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Goroku|qq=}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Masakaze|qq=}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seichūgumi|qq=}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Reforms|qq=}} initiated by [[:en:Shimazu Hisamitsu|Shimazu Hisamitsu]]. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in [[:en:1867|1867]] (Keiō 3). After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], he joined the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] (Department of Shinto Affairs) in [[:en:1871|1871]] (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōbu Daikan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later as a priest of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]].
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kyōsei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 教導職}} and vice-director of the [[:en:Bureau of Shinto Affairs|Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. In [[:en:1882|1882]], he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokoyo Nagatane|qq=}} and [[:en:Senge Takatomi|Senke Sonpuku]] of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo School|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His grave is located at [[:en:Aoyama Cemetery|Aoyama Cemetery]] (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)deity略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)
== References == * Digital Edition: {{ill|Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja|日本人名大辞典+Plus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kodansha) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja|朝日日本歴史人物事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Asahi Shimbun Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Britannica Japan) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Art Dictionary|ja|美術人名事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Art Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shibunkaku Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]]
== External Links == * [http://inakaseikatsu.blogspot.com/2018/03/blog-post_15.html Tanaka Yoritsune and Kokugaku of the Bakumatsu Period – Why Are All the Three Sacred Imperial Tombs of Mythology in Kagoshima? (Part 8) – Nansatsu Nichijō]

== References == * Digital Edition: {{ill|Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja|日本人名大辞典+Plus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kodansha) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja|朝日日本歴史人物事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Asahi Shimbun Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Britannica Japan) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Art Dictionary|ja|美術人名事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Art Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shibunkaku Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]]

revision.comment: Bot: add wikidata link templates
revision.text:
{{Infobox person|birth_date=1836|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|native_name=田中頼庸|given_name={{ill|Yoritsune|qid=Q126082174}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q95162647]] Wikidata name: Tanaka Yoritsune [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[:en:1836|1836]] ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 7) - April 10, [[:en:1897|1897]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道}} scholar, [[:en:Kokugaku|kokugaku]] researcher, and samurai of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]] during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]], Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsunayoshi|qq=|1=Ōyama Tsunayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434686}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeno Yasutsugu|qq=|1=Shigeno Yasutsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6533159}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imafuji Shinzaemon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} while studying at [[:en:Kagoshima Castle|Kagoshima Castle]]. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Goroku|qq=|1=Takasaki Goroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669725}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Masakaze|qq=|1=Takasaki Masakaze|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669778}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seichūgumi|qq=|1=Seichūgumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604673}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Reforms|qq=|1=Bunkyū Reforms|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499741}} initiated by [[:en:Shimazu Hisamitsu|Shimazu Hisamitsu]]. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in [[:en:1867|1867]] (Keiō 3). After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], he joined the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] (Department of Shinto Affairs) in [[:en:1871|1871]] (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōbu Daikan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later as a priest of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]].
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kyōsei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 教導職|1=Kyodo Shoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499314}} and vice-director of the [[:en:Bureau of Shinto Affairs|Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. In [[:en:1882|1882]], he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokoyo Nagatane|qq=|1=Tokoyo Nagatane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536975}} and [[:en:Senge Takatomi|Senke Sonpuku]] of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo School|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His grave is located at [[:en:Aoyama Cemetery|Aoyama Cemetery]] (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)deity略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)
== References == * Digital Edition: {{ill|Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja|日本人名大辞典+Plus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kodansha) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja|朝日日本歴史人物事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Asahi Shimbun Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Britannica Japan) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Art Dictionary|ja|美術人名事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Art Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shibunkaku Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]]
== External Links == * [http://inakaseikatsu.blogspot.com/2018/03/blog-post_15.html Tanaka Yoritsune and Kokugaku of the Bakumatsu Period – Why Are All the Three Sacred Imperial Tombs of Mythology in Kagoshima? (Part 8) – Nansatsu Nichijō]
{{ill|Chief Priest of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮大宮司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief Priest of Ise Grand Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1874–1882)

There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in [[:en:1867|1867]] (Keiō 3). After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], he joined the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] (Department of Shinto Affairs) in [[:en:1871|1871]] (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōbu Daikan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later as a priest of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]].
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kyōsei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 教導職|1=Kyodo Shoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499314}} and vice-director of the [[:en:Bureau of Shinto Affairs|Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. In [[:en:1882|1882]], he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokoyo Nagatane|qq=|1=Tokoyo Nagatane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536975}} and [[:en:Senge Takatomi|Senke Sonpuku]] of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo School|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His grave is located at [[:en:Aoyama Cemetery|Aoyama Cemetery]] (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)deity略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)

revision.text:
{{Infobox person|birth_date=1836|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|native_name=田中頼庸|given_name={{ill|Yoritsune|qid=Q126082174}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q95162647]] Wikidata name: Tanaka Yoritsune [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[:en:1836|1836]] ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 7) - April 10, [[:en:1897|1897]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道}} scholar, [[:en:Kokugaku|kokugaku]] researcher, and samurai of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]] during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]], Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsunayoshi|qq=|1=Ōyama Tsunayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434686}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeno Yasutsugu|qq=|1=Shigeno Yasutsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6533159}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imafuji Shinzaemon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} while studying at [[:en:Kagoshima Castle|Kagoshima Castle]]. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Goroku|qq=|1=Takasaki Goroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669725}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Masakaze|qq=|1=Takasaki Masakaze|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669778}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seichūgumi|qq=|1=Seichūgumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604673}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Reforms|qq=|1=Bunkyū Reforms|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499741}} initiated by [[:en:Shimazu Hisamitsu|Shimazu Hisamitsu]]. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in [[:en:1867|1867]] (Keiō 3). After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], he joined the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] (Department of Shinto Affairs) in [[:en:1871|1871]] (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōbu Daikan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later as a priest of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]].
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kyōsei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 教導職|1=Kyodo Shoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499314}} and vice-director of the [[:en:Bureau of Shinto Affairs|Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. In [[:en:1882|1882]], he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokoyo Nagatane|qq=|1=Tokoyo Nagatane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536975}} and [[:en:Senge Takatomi|Senke Sonpuku]] of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo School|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His grave is located at [[:en:Aoyama Cemetery|Aoyama Cemetery]] (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)deity略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)
== References == * Digital Edition: {{ill|Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja|日本人名大辞典+Plus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kodansha) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja|朝日日本歴史人物事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Asahi Shimbun Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Britannica Japan) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Art Dictionary|ja|美術人名事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Art Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shibunkaku Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]]

== Biography == Born into a family of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]], Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsunayoshi|qq=|1=Ōyama Tsunayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434686}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeno Yasutsugu|qq=|1=Shigeno Yasutsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6533159}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imafuji Shinzaemon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} while studying at [[:en:Kagoshima Castle|Kagoshima Castle]]. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Goroku|qq=|1=Takasaki Goroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669725}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Masakaze|qq=|1=Takasaki Masakaze|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669778}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seichūgumi|qq=|1=Seichūgumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604673}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Reforms|qq=|1=Bunkyū Reforms|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499741}} initiated by [[:en:Shimazu Hisamitsu|Shimazu Hisamitsu]]. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in [[:en:1867|1867]] (Keiō 3). After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], he joined the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] (Department of Shinto Affairs) in [[:en:1871|1871]] (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōbu Daikan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later as a priest of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]].
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kyōsei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 教導職|1=Kyodo Shoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499314}} and vice-director of the [[:en:Bureau of Shinto Affairs|Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. In [[:en:1882|1882]], he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokoyo Nagatane|qq=|1=Tokoyo Nagatane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536975}} and [[:en:Senge Takatomi|Senke Sonpuku]] of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo School|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His grave is located at [[:en:Aoyama Cemetery|Aoyama Cemetery]] (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)deity略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)
== References == * Digital Edition: {{ill|Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja|日本人名大辞典+Plus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kodansha) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja|朝日日本歴史人物事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Asahi Shimbun Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Britannica Japan) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Art Dictionary|ja|美術人名事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Art Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shibunkaku Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]]

After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], he joined the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] (Department of Shinto Affairs) in [[:en:1871|1871]] (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōbu Daikan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later as a priest of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]].
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kyōsei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 教導職|1=Kyodo Shoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499314}} and vice-director of the [[:en:Bureau of Shinto Affairs|Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. In [[:en:1882|1882]], he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokoyo Nagatane|1=Tokoyo Nagatane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536975}} and [[:en:Senge Takatomi|Senke Sonpuku]] of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His grave is located at [[:en:Aoyama Cemetery|Aoyama Cemetery]] (1-ro-17-14).

=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:田中頼庸]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1836|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|native_name=田中頼庸|given_name={{ill|Yoritsune|WD=Q126082174}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q95162647]] Wikidata name: Tanaka Yoritsune [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[:en:1836|1836]] ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 7) - April 10, [[:en:1897|1897]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道}} scholar, [[:en:Kokugaku|kokugaku]] researcher, and samurai of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]] during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]], Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsunayoshi|1=Ōyama Tsunayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434686}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeno Yasutsugu|1=Shigeno Yasutsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6533159}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} while studying at [[:en:Kagoshima Castle|Kagoshima Castle]]. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Goroku|1=Takasaki Goroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669725}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Masakaze|1=Takasaki Masakaze|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669778}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seichūgumi|1=Seichūgumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604673}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Reforms|1=Bunkyū Reforms|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499741}} initiated by [[:en:Shimazu Hisamitsu|Shimazu Hisamitsu]]. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in [[:en:1867|1867]] (Keiō 3). After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], he joined the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] (Department of Shinto Affairs) in [[:en:1871|1871]] (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōbu Daikan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later as a priest of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]].
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kyōsei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 教導職|1=Kyodo Shoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499314}} and vice-director of the [[:en:Bureau of Shinto Affairs|Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. In [[:en:1882|1882]], he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokoyo Nagatane|1=Tokoyo Nagatane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536975}} and [[:en:Senge Takatomi|Senke Sonpuku]] of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His grave is located at [[:en:Aoyama Cemetery|Aoyama Cemetery]] (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)deity略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)
== References == * Digital Edition: {{ill|Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja|日本人名大辞典+Plus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kodansha) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja|朝日日本歴史人物事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Asahi Shimbun Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Britannica Japan) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Art Dictionary|ja|美術人名事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Art Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shibunkaku Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]]
== External Links == * [http://inakaseikatsu.blogspot.com/2018/03/blog-post_15.html Tanaka Yoritsune and Kokugaku of the Bakumatsu Period – Why Are All the Three Sacred Imperial Tombs of Mythology in Kagoshima? (Part 8) – Nansatsu Nichijō]
{{ill|Chief Priest of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮大宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief Priest of Ise Grand Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1874–1882)
{{translated page|ja|田中頼庸|version=103828780|comment=Imported full ja history}}
