<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:田中頼庸]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1836|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|native_name=田中頼庸|given_name={{ill|Yoritsune|WD=Q126082174}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q95162647]] Wikidata name: Tanaka Yoritsune [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[:en:1836|1836]] ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 7) - April 10, [[:en:1897|1897]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道}} scholar, [[:en:Kokugaku|kokugaku]] researcher, and samurai of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]] during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]], Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsunayoshi|1=Ōyama Tsunayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434686}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeno Yasutsugu|1=Shigeno Yasutsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6533159}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} while studying at [[:en:Kagoshima Castle|Kagoshima Castle]]. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Goroku|1=Takasaki Goroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669725}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Masakaze|1=Takasaki Masakaze|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669778}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seichūgumi|1=Seichūgumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604673}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Reforms|1=Bunkyū Reforms|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499741}} initiated by [[:en:Shimazu Hisamitsu|Shimazu Hisamitsu]]. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in [[:en:1867|1867]] (Keiō 3). After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], he joined the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] (Department of Shinto Affairs) in [[:en:1871|1871]] (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōbu Daikan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later as a priest of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]].
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kyōsei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 教導職|1=Kyodo Shoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499314}} and vice-director of the [[:en:Bureau of Shinto Affairs|Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. In [[:en:1882|1882]], he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokoyo Nagatane|1=Tokoyo Nagatane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536975}} and [[:en:Senge Takatomi|Senke Sonpuku]] of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His grave is located at [[:en:Aoyama Cemetery|Aoyama Cemetery]] (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)deity略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:田中頼庸]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1836|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|native_name=田中頼庸|given_name={{ill|Yoritsune|WD=Q126082174}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q95162647]] Wikidata name: Tanaka Yoritsune [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[:en:1836|1836]] ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 7) - April 10, [[:en:1897|1897]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道}} scholar, [[:en:Kokugaku|kokugaku]] researcher, and samurai of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]] during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]], Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsunayoshi|1=Ōyama Tsunayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434686}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeno Yasutsugu|1=Shigeno Yasutsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6533159}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} while studying at [[:en:Kagoshima Castle|Kagoshima Castle]]. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Goroku|1=Takasaki Goroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669725}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Masakaze|1=Takasaki Masakaze|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669778}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seichūgumi|1=Seichūgumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604673}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Reforms|1=Bunkyū Reforms|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499741}} initiated by [[:en:Shimazu Hisamitsu|Shimazu Hisamitsu]]. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in [[:en:1867|1867]] (Keiō 3). After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], he joined the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] (Department of Shinto Affairs) in [[:en:1871|1871]] (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōbu Daikan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later as a priest of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]].
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kyōsei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 教導職|1=Kyodo Shoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499314}} and vice-director of the [[:en:Bureau of Shinto Affairs|Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. In [[:en:1882|1882]], he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokoyo Nagatane|1=Tokoyo Nagatane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536975}} and [[:en:Senge Takatomi|Senke Sonpuku]] of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His grave is located at [[:en:Aoyama Cemetery|Aoyama Cemetery]] (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)deity略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)
== References == * Digital Edition: {{ill|Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja|日本人名大辞典+Plus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kodansha) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja|朝日日本歴史人物事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Asahi Shimbun Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Britannica Japan) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Art Dictionary|ja|美術人名事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Art Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shibunkaku Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]]
== External Links == * [http://inakaseikatsu.blogspot.com/2018/03/blog-post_15.html Tanaka Yoritsune and Kokugaku of the Bakumatsu Period – Why Are All the Three Sacred Imperial Tombs of Mythology in Kagoshima? (Part 8) – Nansatsu Nichijō]
{{ill|Chief Priest of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮大宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief Priest of Ise Grand Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1874–1882)

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:田中頼庸]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1836|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|native_name=田中頼庸|given_name={{ill|Yoritsune|WD=Q126082174}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q95162647]] Wikidata name: Tanaka Yoritsune [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[:en:1836|1836]] ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 7) - April 10, [[:en:1897|1897]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道}} scholar, [[:en:Kokugaku|kokugaku]] researcher, and samurai of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]] during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]], Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsunayoshi|1=Ōyama Tsunayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434686}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeno Yasutsugu|1=Shigeno Yasutsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6533159}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} while studying at [[:en:Kagoshima Castle|Kagoshima Castle]]. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Goroku|1=Takasaki Goroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669725}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Masakaze|1=Takasaki Masakaze|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669778}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seichūgumi|1=Seichūgumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604673}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Reforms|1=Bunkyū Reforms|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499741}} initiated by [[:en:Shimazu Hisamitsu|Shimazu Hisamitsu]]. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in [[:en:1867|1867]] (Keiō 3). After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], he joined the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] (Department of Shinto Affairs) in [[:en:1871|1871]] (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōbu Daikan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later as a priest of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]].
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kyōsei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 教導職|1=Kyodo Shoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499314}} and vice-director of the [[:en:Bureau of Shinto Affairs|Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. In [[:en:1882|1882]], he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokoyo Nagatane|1=Tokoyo Nagatane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536975}} and [[:en:Senge Takatomi|Senke Sonpuku]] of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His grave is located at [[:en:Aoyama Cemetery|Aoyama Cemetery]] (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)deity略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)
== References == * Digital Edition: {{ill|Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja|日本人名大辞典+Plus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kodansha) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja|朝日日本歴史人物事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Asahi Shimbun Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Britannica Japan) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Art Dictionary|ja|美術人名事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Art Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shibunkaku Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]]
== External Links == * [http://inakaseikatsu.blogspot.com/2018/03/blog-post_15.html Tanaka Yoritsune and Kokugaku of the Bakumatsu Period – Why Are All the Three Sacred Imperial Tombs of Mythology in Kagoshima? (Part 8) – Nansatsu Nichijō]
{{ill|Chief Priest of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮大宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief Priest of Ise Grand Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1874–1882)
{{translated page|ja|田中頼庸|version=103828780|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:田中頼庸]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:田中頼庸]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:田中頼庸]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1836|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|native_name=田中頼庸|given_name={{ill|Yoritsune|WD=Q126082174}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q95162647]] Wikidata name: Tanaka Yoritsune [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[:en:1836|1836]] ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 7) - April 10, [[:en:1897|1897]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道}} scholar, [[:en:Kokugaku|kokugaku]] researcher, and samurai of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]] during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]], Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsunayoshi|1=Ōyama Tsunayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434686}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeno Yasutsugu|1=Shigeno Yasutsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6533159}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} while studying at [[:en:Kagoshima Castle|Kagoshima Castle]]. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Goroku|1=Takasaki Goroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669725}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Masakaze|1=Takasaki Masakaze|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669778}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seichūgumi|1=Seichūgumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604673}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Reforms|1=Bunkyū Reforms|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499741}} initiated by [[:en:Shimazu Hisamitsu|Shimazu Hisamitsu]]. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in [[:en:1867|1867]] (Keiō 3). After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], he joined the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] (Department of Shinto Affairs) in [[:en:1871|1871]] (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōbu Daikan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later as a priest of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]].
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kyōsei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 教導職|1=Kyodo Shoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499314}} and vice-director of the [[:en:Bureau of Shinto Affairs|Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. In [[:en:1882|1882]], he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokoyo Nagatane|1=Tokoyo Nagatane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536975}} and [[:en:Senge Takatomi|Senke Sonpuku]] of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His grave is located at [[:en:Aoyama Cemetery|Aoyama Cemetery]] (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)deity略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)
== References == * Digital Edition: {{ill|Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja|日本人名大辞典+Plus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kodansha) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja|朝日日本歴史人物事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Asahi Shimbun Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Britannica Japan) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Art Dictionary|ja|美術人名事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Art Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shibunkaku Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]]
== External Links == * [http://inakaseikatsu.blogspot.com/2018/03/blog-post_15.html Tanaka Yoritsune and Kokugaku of the Bakumatsu Period – Why Are All the Three Sacred Imperial Tombs of Mythology in Kagoshima? (Part 8) – Nansatsu Nichijō]
{{ill|Chief Priest of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮大宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief Priest of Ise Grand Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1874–1882)
{{translated page|ja|田中頼庸|version=103828780|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:田中頼庸]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:田中頼庸]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:田中頼庸]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1836|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|native_name=田中頼庸|given_name={{ill|Yoritsune|WD=Q126082174}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q95162647]] Wikidata name: Tanaka Yoritsune [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[:en:1836|1836]] ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 7) - April 10, [[:en:1897|1897]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道}} scholar, [[:en:Kokugaku|kokugaku]] researcher, and samurai of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]] during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]], Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsunayoshi|1=Ōyama Tsunayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434686}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeno Yasutsugu|1=Shigeno Yasutsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6533159}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} while studying at [[:en:Kagoshima Castle|Kagoshima Castle]]. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Goroku|1=Takasaki Goroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669725}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Masakaze|1=Takasaki Masakaze|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669778}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seichūgumi|1=Seichūgumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604673}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Reforms|1=Bunkyū Reforms|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499741}} initiated by [[:en:Shimazu Hisamitsu|Shimazu Hisamitsu]]. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in [[:en:1867|1867]] (Keiō 3). After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], he joined the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] (Department of Shinto Affairs) in [[:en:1871|1871]] (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōbu Daikan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later as a priest of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]].
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kyōsei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 教導職|1=Kyodo Shoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499314}} and vice-director of the [[:en:Bureau of Shinto Affairs|Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. In [[:en:1882|1882]], he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokoyo Nagatane|1=Tokoyo Nagatane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536975}} and [[:en:Senge Takatomi|Senke Sonpuku]] of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His grave is located at [[:en:Aoyama Cemetery|Aoyama Cemetery]] (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)deity略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)
== References == * Digital Edition: {{ill|Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja|日本人名大辞典+Plus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kodansha) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja|朝日日本歴史人物事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Asahi Shimbun Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Britannica Japan) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Art Dictionary|ja|美術人名事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Art Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shibunkaku Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]]
== External Links == * [http://inakaseikatsu.blogspot.com/2018/03/blog-post_15.html Tanaka Yoritsune and Kokugaku of the Bakumatsu Period – Why Are All the Three Sacred Imperial Tombs of Mythology in Kagoshima? (Part 8) – Nansatsu Nichijō]
{{ill|Chief Priest of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮大宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief Priest of Ise Grand Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1874–1882)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:田中頼庸]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:田中頼庸]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:田中頼庸]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1836|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|native_name=田中頼庸|given_name={{ill|Yoritsune|WD=Q126082174}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q95162647]] Wikidata name: Tanaka Yoritsune [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May [[:en:1836|1836]] ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 7) - April 10, [[:en:1897|1897]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道}} scholar, [[:en:Kokugaku|kokugaku]] researcher, and samurai of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]] during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]], Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsunayoshi|1=Ōyama Tsunayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434686}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeno Yasutsugu|1=Shigeno Yasutsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6533159}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} while studying at [[:en:Kagoshima Castle|Kagoshima Castle]]. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Goroku|1=Takasaki Goroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669725}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Masakaze|1=Takasaki Masakaze|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669778}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seichūgumi|1=Seichūgumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604673}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Reforms|1=Bunkyū Reforms|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499741}} initiated by [[:en:Shimazu Hisamitsu|Shimazu Hisamitsu]]. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in [[:en:1867|1867]] (Keiō 3). After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], he joined the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] (Department of Shinto Affairs) in [[:en:1871|1871]] (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōbu Daikan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later as a priest of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]].
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kyōsei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 教導職|1=Kyodo Shoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499314}} and vice-director of the [[:en:Bureau of Shinto Affairs|Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. In [[:en:1882|1882]], he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokoyo Nagatane|1=Tokoyo Nagatane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536975}} and [[:en:Senge Takatomi|Senke Sonpuku]] of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His grave is located at [[:en:Aoyama Cemetery|Aoyama Cemetery]] (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)deity略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)
== References == * Digital Edition: {{ill|Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja|日本人名大辞典+Plus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kodansha) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja|朝日日本歴史人物事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Asahi Shimbun Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Britannica Japan) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] * {{ill|Art Dictionary|ja|美術人名事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Art Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shibunkaku Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]]
== External Links == * [http://inakaseikatsu.blogspot.com/2018/03/blog-post_15.html Tanaka Yoritsune and Kokugaku of the Bakumatsu Period – Why Are All the Three Sacred Imperial Tombs of Mythology in Kagoshima? (Part 8) – Nansatsu Nichijō]
{{ill|Chief Priest of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮大宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief Priest of Ise Grand Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1874–1882)
{{translated page|ja|田中頼庸|version=103828780|comment=Imported full ja history}}

== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)deity略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)
== References == * Digital Edition: {{ill|Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja|日本人名大辞典+Plus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kodansha) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|en|Kotobank|WD=Q11302260|lt=Kotobank}} * {{ill|Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja|朝日日本歴史人物事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Asahi Shimbun Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|en|Kotobank|WD=Q11302260|lt=Kotobank}} * {{ill|Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Britannica Japan) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|en|Kotobank|WD=Q11302260|lt=Kotobank}} * {{ill|Art Dictionary|ja|美術人名事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Art Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shibunkaku Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|en|Kotobank|WD=Q11302260|lt=Kotobank}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:田中頼庸]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:田中頼庸]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:田中頼庸]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1836|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|native_name=田中頼庸|given_name={{ill|Yoritsune|WD=Q126082174}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q95162647]] Wikidata name: Tanaka Yoritsune [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May {{ill|1836|en|1836|WD=Q7601|lt=1836}} ({{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō}} 7) - April 10, {{ill|1897|en|1897|WD=Q7847|lt=1897}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道}} scholar, {{ill|Kokugaku|en|Kokugaku|WD=Q855159|lt=kokugaku}} researcher, and samurai of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|en|Satsuma Domain|WD=Q676187|lt=Satsuma Domain}} during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|en|Satsuma Domain|WD=Q676187|lt=Satsuma Domain}}, Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsunayoshi|1=Ōyama Tsunayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434686}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeno Yasutsugu|1=Shigeno Yasutsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6533159}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} while studying at {{ill|Kagoshima Castle|en|Kagoshima Castle|WD=Q1066957|lt=Kagoshima Castle}}. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Goroku|1=Takasaki Goroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669725}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Masakaze|1=Takasaki Masakaze|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669778}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seichūgumi|1=Seichūgumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604673}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Reforms|1=Bunkyū Reforms|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499741}} initiated by {{ill|Shimazu Hisamitsu|en|Shimazu Hisamitsu|WD=Q656135|lt=Shimazu Hisamitsu}}. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in {{ill|1867|en|1867|WD=Q7715|lt=1867}} (Keiō 3). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, he joined the {{ill|Ministry of Divinities|en|Ministry of Divinities|WD=Q4294696|lt=Department of Divinities}} (Department of Shinto Affairs) in {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871}} (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōbu Daikan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later as a priest of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine}}.
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kyōsei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 教導職|1=Kyodo Shoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499314}} and vice-director of the {{ill|Bureau of Shinto Affairs|en|Bureau of Shinto Affairs|WD=Q11590918|lt=Bureau of Shinto Affairs}}. In {{ill|1882|en|1882|WD=Q7812|lt=1882}}, he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokoyo Nagatane|1=Tokoyo Nagatane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536975}} and {{ill|Senge Takatomi|en|Senge Takatomi|WD=Q11405449|lt=Senke Sonpuku}} of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His grave is located at {{ill|Aoyama Cemetery|en|Aoyama Cemetery|WD=Q1036028|lt=Aoyama Cemetery}} (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)deity略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)
== References == * Digital Edition: {{ill|Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja|日本人名大辞典+Plus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kodansha) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|en|Kotobank|WD=Q11302260|lt=Kotobank}} * {{ill|Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja|朝日日本歴史人物事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Asahi Shimbun Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|en|Kotobank|WD=Q11302260|lt=Kotobank}} * {{ill|Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Britannica Japan) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|en|Kotobank|WD=Q11302260|lt=Kotobank}} * {{ill|Art Dictionary|ja|美術人名事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Art Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shibunkaku Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|en|Kotobank|WD=Q11302260|lt=Kotobank}}
== External Links == * [http://inakaseikatsu.blogspot.com/2018/03/blog-post_15.html Tanaka Yoritsune and Kokugaku of the Bakumatsu Period – Why Are All the Three Sacred Imperial Tombs of Mythology in Kagoshima? (Part 8) – Nansatsu Nichijō]
{{ill|Chief Priest of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮大宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief Priest of Ise Grand Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1874–1882)

From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeno Yasutsugu|1=Shigeno Yasutsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6533159}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} while studying at {{ill|Kagoshima Castle|en|Kagoshima Castle|WD=Q1066957|lt=Kagoshima Castle}}. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Goroku|1=Takasaki Goroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669725}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Masakaze|1=Takasaki Masakaze|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669778}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seichūgumi|1=Seichūgumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604673}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Reforms|1=Bunkyū Reforms|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499741}} initiated by {{ill|Shimazu Hisamitsu|en|Shimazu Hisamitsu|WD=Q656135|lt=Shimazu Hisamitsu}}. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in {{ill|1867|en|1867|WD=Q7715|lt=1867}} (Keiō 3). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, he joined the {{ill|Ministry of Divinities|en|Ministry of Divinities|WD=Q4294696|lt=Department of Divinities}} (Department of Shinto Affairs) in {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871}} (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōbu Daikan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later as a priest of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine}}.
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kyōsei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 教導職|1=Kyodo Shoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499314}} and vice-director of the {{ill|Bureau of Shinto Affairs|en|Bureau of Shinto Affairs|WD=Q11590918|lt=Bureau of Shinto Affairs}}. In {{ill|1882|en|1882|WD=Q7812|lt=1882}}, he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokoyo Nagatane|1=Tokoyo Nagatane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536975}} and {{ill|Senge Takatomi|en|Senge Takatomi|WD=Q11405449|lt=Senke Sonpuku}} of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His grave is located at {{ill|Aoyama Cemetery|en|Aoyama Cemetery|WD=Q1036028|lt=Aoyama Cemetery}} (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)deity略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)
== References == * Digital Edition: {{ill|Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja|日本人名大辞典+Plus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kodansha) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|en|Kotobank|WD=Q11302260|lt=Kotobank}} * {{ill|Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja|朝日日本歴史人物事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Asahi Shimbun Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|en|Kotobank|WD=Q11302260|lt=Kotobank}} * {{ill|Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Britannica Japan) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|en|Kotobank|WD=Q11302260|lt=Kotobank}} * {{ill|Art Dictionary|ja|美術人名事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Art Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shibunkaku Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|en|Kotobank|WD=Q11302260|lt=Kotobank}}

After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, he joined the {{ill|Ministry of Divinities|en|Ministry of Divinities|WD=Q4294696|lt=Department of Divinities}} (Department of Shinto Affairs) in {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871}} (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōbu Daikan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later as a priest of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:田中頼庸]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:田中頼庸]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:田中頼庸]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1836|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}|native_name=田中頼庸|given_name={{ill|Yoritsune|WD=Q126082174|1=Yoritsune}}}} '''Tanaka Yoritsune''' (田中 頼庸, May {{ill|1836|en|1836|WD=Q7601|lt=1836|1=1836}} ({{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō|1=Tenpō}} 7) - April 10, {{ill|1897|en|1897|WD=Q7847|lt=1897|1=1897}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 30)) was a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道}} scholar, {{ill|Kokugaku|en|Kokugaku|WD=Q855159|lt=kokugaku|1=Kokugaku}} researcher, and samurai of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|en|Satsuma Domain|WD=Q676187|lt=Satsuma Domain|1=Satsuma Domain}} during the late Edo and early Meiji periods. His common name was Tōhachi, and he was also known by the pen names Unsū and Umeya.
== Biography == Born into a family of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|en|Satsuma Domain|WD=Q676187|lt=Satsuma Domain|1=Satsuma Domain}}, Tanaka was a nephew of {{ill|Ōyama Tsunayoshi|ja|大山綱良|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Tsunayoshi|1=Ōyama Tsunayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434686}}. From a young age, he became a samurai of the Satsuma Domain and was recognized for his literary talents alongside figures such as {{ill|Shigeno Yasutsugu|ja|重野安繹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeno Yasutsugu|1=Shigeno Yasutsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6533159}} and {{ill|Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja|今藤新左衛門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imafuji Shinzaemon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} while studying at {{ill|Kagoshima Castle|en|Kagoshima Castle|WD=Q1066957|lt=Kagoshima Castle|1=Kagoshima Castle}}. Through his friendship with {{ill|Takasaki Goroku|ja|高崎五六|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Goroku|1=Takasaki Goroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669725}}, a cousin of {{ill|Takasaki Masakaze|ja|高崎正風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki Masakaze|1=Takasaki Masakaze|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669778}}, Yoritsune was invited to join the {{ill|Seichūgumi|ja|精忠組|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seichūgumi|1=Seichūgumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604673}}, a group of young Satsuma samurai, but he declined.
Later, Tanaka moved to {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}} as part of the {{ill|Bunkyū Reforms|ja|文久の改革|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Reforms|1=Bunkyū Reforms|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499741}} initiated by {{ill|Shimazu Hisamitsu|en|Shimazu Hisamitsu|WD=Q656135|lt=Shimazu Hisamitsu|1=Shimazu Hisamitsu}}. There, he studied Confucianism, medicine, and other subjects. Subsequently, he specialized in kokugaku and returned to Satsuma in {{ill|1867|en|1867|WD=Q7715|lt=1867|1=1867}} (Keiō 3). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, he joined the {{ill|Ministry of Divinities|en|Ministry of Divinities|WD=Q4294696|lt=Department of Divinities|1=Ministry of Divinities}} (Department of Shinto Affairs) in {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871|1=1871}} (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōbu Daikan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later as a priest of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}.
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kyōsei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 教導職|1=Kyodo Shoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499314}} and vice-director of the {{ill|Bureau of Shinto Affairs|en|Bureau of Shinto Affairs|WD=Q11590918|lt=Bureau of Shinto Affairs|1=Bureau of Shinto Affairs}}. In {{ill|1882|en|1882|WD=Q7812|lt=1882|1=1882}}, he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokoyo Nagatane|1=Tokoyo Nagatane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536975}} and {{ill|Senge Takatomi|en|Senge Takatomi|WD=Q11405449|lt=Senke Sonpuku|1=Senge Takatomi}} of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His grave is located at {{ill|Aoyama Cemetery|en|Aoyama Cemetery|WD=Q1036028|lt=Aoyama Cemetery|1=Aoyama Cemetery}} (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)deity略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)
== References == * Digital Edition: {{ill|Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja|日本人名大辞典+Plus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Biographical Dictionary+Plus|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kodansha) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|en|Kotobank|WD=Q11302260|lt=Kotobank|1=Kotobank}} * {{ill|Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja|朝日日本歴史人物事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Dictionary of Historical Figures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Asahi Shimbun Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|en|Kotobank|WD=Q11302260|lt=Kotobank|1=Kotobank}} * {{ill|Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Britannica International Encyclopedia (Small Entry)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Britannica Japan) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|en|Kotobank|WD=Q11302260|lt=Kotobank|1=Kotobank}} * {{ill|Art Dictionary|ja|美術人名事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Art Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shibunkaku Publishing) “[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%AD%E9%A0%BC%E5%BA%B8-18771 Tanaka Yoritsune]” - {{ill|Kotobank|en|Kotobank|WD=Q11302260|lt=Kotobank|1=Kotobank}}
== External Links == * [http://inakaseikatsu.blogspot.com/2018/03/blog-post_15.html Tanaka Yoritsune and Kokugaku of the Bakumatsu Period – Why Are All the Three Sacred Imperial Tombs of Mythology in Kagoshima? (Part 8) – Nansatsu Nichijō]
{{ill|Chief Priest of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮大宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief Priest of Ise Grand Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1874–1882)

After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, he joined the {{ill|Ministry of Divinities|en|Ministry of Divinities|WD=Q4294696|lt=Department of Divinities|1=Ministry of Divinities}} (Department of Shinto Affairs) in {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871|1=1871}} (Meiji 4), serving as a {{ill|Kyōbu Daikan|ja|教部大録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōbu Daikan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later as a priest of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}.
Tanaka continued his career, becoming a {{ill|Dai Kyōsei|ja|大教正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kyōsei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 教導職|1=Kyodo Shoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499314}} and vice-director of the {{ill|Bureau of Shinto Affairs|en|Bureau of Shinto Affairs|WD=Q11590918|lt=Bureau of Shinto Affairs|1=Bureau of Shinto Affairs}}. In {{ill|1882|en|1882|WD=Q7812|lt=1882|1=1882}}, he resigned from his position as a priest after being promoted to director, becoming independent. During this time, he became well-known for his criticism of figures such as {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokoyo Nagatane|1=Tokoyo Nagatane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536975}} and {{ill|Senge Takatomi|en|Senge Takatomi|WD=Q11405449|lt=Senke Sonpuku|1=Senge Takatomi}} of the {{ill|Izumo School|ja|出雲派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His grave is located at {{ill|Aoyama Cemetery|en|Aoyama Cemetery|WD=Q1036028|lt=Aoyama Cemetery|1=Aoyama Cemetery}} (1-ro-17-14).
== Major Works == === Principal Works === * *Kōtei Nihonki* (『校訂日本紀』) * *Kōtei Kojiki* (『校訂古事記』) * *Shintokuron* (『神徳論』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Ryakki* (『(神宮)deity略記』)
=== Other Works === * *Sansoku Engi* (『三則演義』) * *Ume no Ya Bunshū* (『梅の屋文集』) * *Kōtei Kogo Shūi* (『校訂古語拾遺』) * *(Jingū) Saijin Teiyō* (『(神宮)deity提要』) * *Gosho* (『語書』)
